reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider that GLOW (BPC-157 + TB-500 + GHK-Cu) is a contested blend: Hunter and Taylor frame it as a synergistic healing/anti-aging stack while Trevor calls combined-peptide vials biologically incoherent. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh whether the patient's goal is short-cycle injury/skin recovery (Hunter and Taylor's frame) or controlled-variable peptide research (Trevor's frame), and to surface Hunter's hard chemistry rule that vitamin C and glutathione must NEVER be co-mixed with GHK-Cu in the same vial. Newer Hunter content (2026-03-13) leans toward rotating the components rather than blending them, which the practitioner should weigh against the 2025-07-26 GLOW dosing protocol.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- NEVER co-mix vitamin C or glutathione with GHK-Cu: Hunter (threadId 19bc90e12abefa74, 2026-01-16): "DO NOT mix vitamin C OR glutathione into the same vial as GHK-Cu" and "Convenient? Yes — 'two of the worst shots in one shot.' NOT worth it. Will mess with copper chemistry."
- GHK-Cu burns at injection site for some users: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:16:41.110): "I do this even if I'm just taking ghk by itself because for me it does burn"; her workaround is dilution to 6 mL total in a larger vial.
- Receptor saturation / overdose risk in stacks: Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:38:21.040): "I see so many people overdose where you don't need a really high amount" and Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:38:54.400): "more isn't always better."
- Trevor's blanket caveat against blends: Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:43:15.119): "They're not in the same vial. That's for idiots"; Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:43:31.040): "receptor competition. They compete for the same binding site. Conflicting signals"; Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:44:09.040): "specifically with GHKCU, it chilates copper, which will interact with any other metal binding peptide."
- Marketing-frame skepticism even from Hunter: Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 01:55:14.000): "I think the the glow the word the glow is like the best has been the best marketing strategy because every woman hears the glow blend and it's really more the wolverine like a healing blend."
- Cloudiness/precipitation = don't use: Hunter (threadId 19bc90e12abefa74, 2026-01-16): "If you see cloudiness or particulates → don't use it (basic common sense)."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts agree GLOW = BPC-157 + TB-500 + GHK-Cu as the component identity. Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "the GLOW blend (50mg GHK-Cu, 10mg BPC-157, 10mg TB-500 per vial)"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:02:23.319): "the glow stack is essentially bpc um 157 tb500 and ghk cuu the copper peptide"; Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:43:09.839): "Don't you sell the Wolverine stack or the glow or the glow?" — confirming the same three-peptide identity. Both Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26) and Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:02:43.190) frame it as a healing/recovery stack: Hunter says "Faster recovery from injuries: Tendons, ligaments, and muscle tears heal in record time" and Taylor says "the Wolverine stack so it's the ultimate like healing blend." Hunter and Taylor both teach the SAME reconstitution geometry: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "Add 2ml of bacteriostatic water to your vial"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:14:47.800): "2 mL of water going into this" — 2 mL bac water into a 10/10/50 mg blend vial.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Whole-blend vs separate-vial is the headline split. Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:42:58.240): "They sell peptides. They sell blends. Clo glow, all the stuff." Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:43:11.520): "All separate, baby. Bet your butt I do. But they're all separate. They're not in the same vial. That's for idiots." Trevor (XUdc1evyaFg 00:43:28.800): "When you take multiple peptides simultaneously, receptor competition. They compete for the same binding site. Conflicting signals." Hunter explicitly defends blends in writing — Hunter (threadId 19bc90e12abefa74, 2026-01-16): "these blends are stable, and yes, they work" and "BPC-157, TB-500, KPV are small water-soluble peptides → far less aggregation risk" and "21-day degradation test post-reconstitution: 2-3% degradation across components. Not zero. Not dead. Not worthless. Normal peptide behavior." Taylor sells/teaches the blend as a single workflow — Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:14:09.320): "reconstitute my bpc dv500 blend and I'm going to be putting 2 m of water into" it. Hunter himself shifts in newer content — Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:36:21.760): "I actually like keeping them separate. And I think in the short term they're probably okay" and Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 00:37:09.120): "I would actually prefer if I'm using those for a certain effect to rotate through them almost like I'm going to do BPC maybe even if it's just like a weekly thing BPC for a week then TB the next week and then GHK the next week." Newer Hunter (2026-03-13) overrides older Hunter (2025-07-26) on his own preference, but the 2025-07-26 protocol remains operative for users who choose to blend. Dosing also differs: Hunter prescribes 10 units (0.1 mL) once daily — Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily, morning or evening, your choice." Taylor uses a different reconstitution — Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:28:01.559): "take um 60 units of it" of the diluted GHK-Cu (her 6 mL dilution) for 5 days a week.

acute.dose: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "the GLOW blend (50mg GHK-Cu, 10mg BPC-157, 10mg TB-500 per vial)" with "Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily."
acute.route: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "For systemic benefit (whole-body recovery, general anti-aging), inject subcutaneously in the belly fat. For targeted healing (painful knees, shoulder, surgery site), inject near the affected area (but not directly into the injury—always around, not in)."
acute.frequency: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "once daily, morning or evening, your choice."
acute.duration: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "You can run this for 20 days (one vial per cycle), and repeat as needed, especially during injury recovery or periods of intense training or stress."
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter (threadId 198489a54128a775, 2025-07-26): "Reconstitution: Add 2ml of bacteriostatic water to your vial. Dosing: Each 10 units on an insulin syringe = 0.1ml. That gives you a perfect daily dose with 20 doses per vial. Administration: Inject 10 units (0.1ml) once daily."

chronic.dose: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:28:01.559): "take um 60 units of it" referring to her diluted GHK-Cu (50 mg in 6 mL) plus Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:28:15.279): "I would take 20 units of the BBC tv500 and I would take um 15 units of the ghk" when drawn from the original vial, equating to Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:15:23.040): "need 2.5 milligrams" of GHK-Cu and Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:16:14.600): "500 microgr" each of BPC-157 and TB-500 per dose.
chronic.route: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:04:52.199): "a great peptide to use whether you're injecting it or using it topically"; subcutaneous injection is the dominant taught route in both Hunter and Taylor's protocols.
chronic.frequency: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:27:30.200): "a dose amount for 5 days a week of this blend."
chronic.duration: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:26:45.240): "with this blend I would say a good 12 6 to 12 weeks on it is for you to see like the most maximum results."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:25:06.480): "what you're going to notice in about 3 to 6 weeks is you'll notice like a little bit more firmer skin"; Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:26:21.560): "week 6 to 12 on this blend you will notice um more collagen production going on."

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:03:45.670): "I think the glow stack right now is just a very hot topic word with the ladies and because when we hear glow automatically we think of glowing skin" — but Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:02:53.159): "this is what I have used for healing like any major tears" and (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:03:08.560): "one of my girlfriends she used it when she had her implants removed she used it um during that healing time." Taylor (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:27:00.120): "I noticed some really big improvements in my hair growth" with the caveat (LvqyX5QWQZc 00:27:08.320): "body hair growth definitely like will go up with this for me anyway." Hunter explicitly flags the women-marketing frame — Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 01:55:14.000): "I think the the glow the word the glow is like the best has been the best marketing strategy because every woman hears the glow blend" and Hunter (PEU3ZE5axiQ 01:55:31.280): "all go out and go crazy about it for making them have more glowy healthier skin." No fertility, pregnancy, or contraindication-specific signal surfaced in the indexed Taylor content for GLOW; Taylor's framing is cosmetic/healing in adult women, not female-physiology-specific.
